mission.title: spire: spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request / Enhancement for node attestation
mission.description: spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request / Enhancement for node attestation . This issue affects 4+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request / Enhancement for node attestation

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
Hello Team 👋 
Hope you are well.

We are looking for a feature, where in we add one more check to node attestation flow. When this feature is enabled, node can only be attested only if the `account i

steps.title: Apply the fix for spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request / Enhancem
steps.description:
**Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**
This PR adds new feature to AWS Node Attester Plugin. It's optional feature, if its enabled it provides additional check executed at very first step during node attestation. It verifies, if node's `aws account id` is part of `AWS Organization` 
```yaml
account_ids_belong_to_org_validation = {
        org_account_id     = "7891011"
        org_account_role   = "spire-server-org-role"
        org_account_region = "us-west-2"
        org_account_map_ttl = "6"
      }
```

steps.title: Confirm spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: **Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**
This PR adds new feature to AWS Node Attester Plugin. It's optional feature, if its enabled it provides additional check executed at very first step during node attestation. It verifies, if node's `aws account id` is part of `AWS Organization` or not. If it's not part of organization, attestation request will be rejected.

More on this featur. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
org_account_role   = "spire-server-org-role" <---- use same role for node attestation 
        org_account_region = "us-west-2" <---- default region we agree on
        org_account_map_ttl = "6" <--- default ttl if we go with existing route

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
